votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the length check and the element-wise derivative calculation specified in the Coq code.
The Python code correctly implements the length check using max(0, len(xs) - 1) which matches Coq's pred, and accurately checks the derivative calculation for each coefficient.
The Python code correctly checks the length and the elements according to the Coq specification, including the correct handling of the predecessor of the length.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec: it checks that result length equals pred(length xs) (which is max(0, len(xs)-1) in Python), and for each index i < len(result), it verifies result[i] == xs[i+1] * (i+1). The Coq uses nth with default 0, but since i < len(result) implies i+1 < len(xs), the access xs[i+1] is always valid. Z.of_nat(S i) equals i+1 in Python. Both return the same judgment on all concrete inputs.

The Coq uses nth with default 0, but since i < len(result) implies i+1 < len(xs), the access xs[i+1] is always valid. Z.of_nat(S i) equals i+1 in Python integers.
